description: Main learning videos for solving word problems and multi-step problems using real-life village data

videos.title: Word Problems with Village Data | Read, Understand, Solve
videos.description: Learn the steps to solve word problems: read carefully, find the important numbers, decide whether to add or subtract, solve, and check. Use real village data to practice!
learningObjectives: Follow a step-by-step approach to word problems
Identify key information in a problem
Choose the correct operation based on context

questions.question: What is the first step when solving a word problem?
options: Read carefully and find the important information

videos.title: Multi-Step Problems | When You Need More Than One Calculation
videos.description: Some village problems need two steps to solve! First add, then subtract. Or first find a total, then compare. Learn how to break big problems into small steps.
learningObjectives: Recognise when a problem needs more than one step
Break complex problems into smaller parts
Solve two-step word problems with village data

questions.question: A village has 230 mango trees and 145 neem trees. They planted 75 more neem trees. How many neem trees are there now?

videos.title: Solving Village Problems Step by Step | Detailed Worked Examples
videos.description: Watch detailed solutions to village word problems. See how to underline important words, identify the question, choose operations, calculate, and verify your answer.
learningObjectives: See complete worked-out solutions to village problems
Learn to verify answers by checking
Develop a systematic approach to problem solving

videos.title: Problem Solving Challenge | Village Data Word Problems
videos.description: Challenge yourself with village word problems! Each problem uses real data. Pause, solve, and then check your answer. Can you get them all right?

videos.title: Be a Village Problem Solver! | Using Maths to Help Your Community
videos.description: Imagine you are in charge of keeping a village clean. Use addition, subtraction, and data to plan projects, count supplies, and solve real community problems!
learningObjectives: Apply all chapter skills to a real community scenario
Use data to make decisions for a village
See yourself as a problem solver who helps the community

standard.description: For students who scored >= 80% on readiness

with_prerequisites.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness

questions.question: A village collected 215 bags of rubbish on Monday and 187 bags on Tuesday. They sent 320 bags to the recycling centre. How many bags are still in the village?
questions.explanation: Step 1: Total bags = 215 + 187 = 402. Step 2: Bags remaining = 402 - 320 = 82 bags still in the village.

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for the challenge problems

ifWrong.message: Multi-step problems need careful work. Watch the video again and try breaking the problem into two separate steps.

usageNotes.purpose: Develop word problem solving skills using real village data and multi-step scenarios
usageNotes.recommendationStrategy: Select playlist path based on readiness assessment score
usageNotes.sequencing: Word problem strategies → Multi-step problems → Worked examples → Independent challenge → Community problem solver
